title: Past Boards
text:
This page will list the composition of previous RC42 boards.

Information about past RC42 boards is being compiled. If you have served on a previous board or have records of past board compositions, please contact the current board.



The following board terms are known to have existed. Details will be added as they are confirmed:

Board 2018–2023
Board 2014–2018
Board 2010–2014
Earlier terms (since establishment in 1988)


RC42 boards are elected at each ISA World Congress of Sociology, which takes place every four years.

title: Discussions
text:
RC42 Social Psychology recognizes the value of open scholarly discussion and community exchange.



Discussions are not active yet. This section is reserved for a future discussion space that the RC42 board may evaluate in upcoming phases.




The RC42 board may evaluate the following platforms for community discussions, depending on member interest and moderation capacity:

GitHub Discussions — Integrated with the site’s repository; suitable for structured, topic-based threads.
giscus — A comment system powered by GitHub Discussions, allowing page-level comments.
Discord — Real-time messaging; suitable for informal exchanges and community building.
Discourse — A full-featured forum platform; requires dedicated hosting and moderation.




A discussion space should only be activated if:

Sustained participation — There is demonstrated interest from a sufficient number of members to maintain active, meaningful exchanges.
Moderation capacity — There are volunteers or designated moderators willing and able to maintain a respectful, scholarly environment.
Clear purpose — The discussion space serves a need not already met by email, conferences, or other existing channels.
Sustainability — The chosen platform can be maintained over time without excessive technical or administrative burden.




For questions, suggestions, or discussions, members are encouraged to:

Contact the RC42 board at isa-rc42@isa-sociology.org
Engage during RC42 sessions at ISA conferences
Use the contribution form to share ideas


This page will be updated if and when a discussion platform is adopted by the RC42 board.

title: ISA World Congress 2027
text:
The International Sociological Association (ISA) organizes the World Congress of Sociology every four years. The next congress will take place in 2027.
RC42 Social Psychology will participate actively by organizing dedicated sessions, panels, and a business meeting.


The RC42 program for the 2027 Congress is coordinated by the RC42 President:

Juan Carlos Castillo (Universidad de Chile, Chile)





Information regarding the overarching theme, call for sessions, and call for abstracts will be published here as soon as they are announced by the ISA Executive Committee.

Return to Activities

title: About RC42 Social Psychology
text:
ABOUT RC42


A Research Committee of the International Sociological Association, established in 1988, dedicated to international research, debate, and collaboration in sociological social psychology.



Overview Objectives History Relation with ISA Statutes




Overview


RC42 Social Psychology is a Research Committee of the International Sociological Association. It brings together scholars from across the globe who share an interest in the sociological approach to social psychology. The committee focuses on identities, social interaction, group processes, emotions, attitudes, collective behavior, socialization, and the relationship between social structures and individual experience.




Objectives


The aim of RC42 is to contribute to the development of theory and research in social psychology on an international level, uniting professional and scientific interests of its members to work together on a local, regional, and international level.
To achieve these goals, the committee focuses on the following key areas:

International Research: Advancing sociological social psychology internationally by promoting the development, dissemination, and exchange of theory and research across countries and regions.
Sociological Theory & Social Psychology: Bridging diverse theoretical and methodological traditions within sociological social psychology, promoting dialogue across perspectives such as symbolic interactionism, social structure and personality, and group processes.
Scholarly Collaboration: Fostering academic exchange, uniting the professional and scientific interests of members to work at local, regional, and international levels.
Regional & International Coordination: Organizing academic sessions and events, planning and coordinating sessions at ISA World Congresses of Sociology and other relevant international conferences.
Active Participation: Supporting emerging scholars and enhancing the visibility of sociological social psychology within the broader discipline of sociology and across the social sciences.





History


RC42 Social Psychology was established in 1988 as a Research Committee of the International Sociological Association (ISA).
Since its founding, RC42 has served as an institutional home and forum for sociologists interested in social psychology to connect, collaborate, and advance the field at an international level. As part of the network of Research Committees of the ISA, its core function is to promote a community of research in sociological social psychology, facilitating international collaboration and the exchange of scientific knowledge.
For information on previous RC42 boards and their contributions over the decades, see Past Boards.




Relation with ISA


RC42 Social Psychology operates as a recognized Research Committee of the International Sociological Association (ISA).
Please note the following institutional relationship:

Official Membership: Official membership in RC42 is managed and administered through the ISA.
Complementary Portal: This website operates as a complementary digital portal to improve the visibility, circulation of information, and academic collaboration among members.
Institutional Reference: The official ISA RC42 page remains the primary institutional reference for official records.





Statutes


The statutes define the nature, aims, activities, membership rules, governing bodies, board obligations, and general conditions of RC42 Social Psychology.


RC42 is a body of ISA and it supports the development of theory and empirical research in social psychology.
RC42 was formed in accordance with Article XI, Paragraph 1 of the Statutes of ISA, by the decision of the Executive Committee of ISA (EC of ISA).
RC42 reports its activities to the Research Council of ISA.
For the realization of its activities, RC42 obtains funds of its own and spends them according to Article XI, Paragraph 7 of the Statutes of ISA.



The aim of RC42 is to contribute to the development of theory and research in social psychology on an international level, uniting professional and scientific interests of its members to work together on a local, regional, and international level.



For the achievement of the aims stated in Article II of the present Statutes, RC42 supports the following activities:

Exchange of information among its members.
International scientific communication.
International research projects.
Organizational activities and implementation of an international network of social psychologists.

The exchange of information is to be accomplished by publication activities and a documentation system. An information bulletin or newsletter is published in order to inform members and the scientific communities about the ongoing activities of the Research Committee.
International scientific communication about social psychological research is carried out by means of scientific conferences (including jointly sponsored conferences), symposia, colloquia, and other forms of interpersonal communication, etc. of RC42, and by participation in the activities of ISA.
Initiative planning and carrying out of regional and inter-regional projects aim at:

Theoretical research on the development of social psychological theory.
Empirical research connected with key social psychological issues.
Applied research relating social psychology to social policy (e.g. in domains like work, family and health, etc.).
Development, evaluation, and improvement of research methodologies related to social psychology.

The aims of the organizational activities are:

Realization of effective interaction among the social psychologists united by RC42 on the basis of a network model on the international, national, and regional levels.
Realization of effective coordination between RC42 and the other Research Committees of the ISA.
Realization of effective coordination between RC42 and the governing bodies of ISA.
Realization of effective cooperation and coordination between the relevant institutions and organizations in connection with the advancement of the field of social psychology.




Members of RC42 can be individuals or institutions:

Scientists dealing with social psychology by teaching and/or by conducting research, or related scientific professions.
Organizations dealing with social psychology and corresponding national associations.
Other scientific international or regional-level organizations dealing with social psychology.

All members of RC42 are entitled to:

Vote as individual members in the discussions about organizational matters at the General Meetings according to the principle “one member, one vote”; that means that one institution also has one vote.
Participate in the scientific activities of the Committee.
Receive the Information Bulletin for Social Psychology (IBSP) or Newsletter free of charge.

Members of RC42 are obliged to:

Uphold the present statutes of RC42 and to adhere to the stipulations of the present statutes.
Conscientiously fulfill the tasks entrusted to them by the General Meeting or other bodies of RC42.
Pay their fee for one period (4 years) that is:

20 U.S. dollars or equivalent for individual members. If payment is not possible, exceptions can be made by the Board in accordance with ISA.
200 U.S. dollars or equivalent for institutional members.


The Board of RC42 shall discuss the state of membership each year and shall make the corresponding decisions regarding active membership.



For the effective fulfillment of its activities, RC42 forms the following bodies:

General Meeting
Board or Executive Council
Editorial Committee
Advisory Board



The General Meeting comprises all members who have the right to vote. The General Meeting is held:

Regularly during the congresses of ISA;
At the proposal of one third of its members of the General Meeting;
General Meetings have to be announced at least six months in advance.

The General Meeting:

Determines the directions of activities and accepts the plan for the work of the Committee;
Set policies and elect a nominating committee to take responsibility for the election of the Board of the Committee for the following term;
The decisions of the General Meeting are taken by a simple majority (50% of the participating members plus one) by open vote.




The Board of RC42 consists of 8 members as follows:

President
Secretary /Treasurer
Editor-in-Chief of the editions of the Newsletter of RC42
Four regional representatives
Ex-President of the Committee

The Board or Executive Council of RC42:

Elaborates the Plan of Activities of the Committee;
Organizes the fulfillment of the Plan of Activities of the Committee (scientific activities conferences, symposia, colloquia, and other forms of interpersonal communication, publishing of the Information Bulletin, international research projects, etc.);
Promotes scientific and organizational contacts with other research committees of ISA;
If necessary, it creates sub-committees for the realization of the activities of the Committee (e.g. program committees);
It establishes contacts with the governing bodies of ISA;
It reports its activities to the General Meeting;
It reports activities of the Committee to the governing bodies of ISA.




All board members must be in good standing in both the RC and ISA throughout the four years of their mandate.
The Board of RC42 is elected every four years for a four-year term between each World Congress of Sociology, with their term to commence at the following World Congress.
At the required world congress general membership meeting, members of RC42 network, set policies and elect a nominating committee consisting of a Chair and members representative of the different regions of RC42 membership. The nominating committee issues a call and solicits nominations both at the general meeting and in the newsletter and receives nominations for all the offices listed above and proposes two candidates for each position. The list of nominees is circulated in a newsletter to all members with a call for additional nominations. The Chair of the nominations committee administers the election by mail ballot, and officers are elected from a list of nominated candidates on the basis of a simple majority of ballots received by the deadline specified on the ballot. In the case of a tie between candidates, the issue is resolved in a manner that is mindful of regional representation, and for candidates from the same region, by drawing lots.
A member may be a candidate for more than one office but may only serve in one office at a time. A meeting of members from both the old and new RC42 Boards is held at the beginning of the World Congress of Sociology to help evaluate past activities, to establish planning committees and to begin to plan activities for the next four years. This meeting is open to all RC42 members. Officially the new officers begin their mandate at the business meeting after the nominating committee report. In the transition phase, all serving officers who so wish will continue their term until the balloting is finalized and the nominating committee has declared them elected at the next membership meeting. Interim positions will be filled by the Chair.
No officer may serve more than two consecutive full terms in the same position.
The President may not be re-elected for the following term.
In the event that an officer is unable to serve, the Chair may appoint a replacement, after consultation with other appropriate officers.
The Board of RC42 makes decisions at its meetings by a quorum of 50% plus one of the members and by an open vote ballot with a simple majority (50% plus one of the participants).



The Editorial Committee assists the Board in the realization of the publication activities of RC42. It promotes contacts with the publishing house of ISA (SAGE).



The Advisory Board of RC42 will be formed of prominent social psychologists. The members of the Advisory Board will be invited to join by the President and the Past-president. Their membership will be approved by the Board.







Presides at the General Meeting and the Meeting of the Board of RC42.
Represents RC42 as a delegate to the Research Council of ISA and other organizations.
Conducts the activities of the Board of RC42 in accordance with the decisions of the General Meeting.
Is responsible for the activities of the Board to the General Meeting of RC42.
Is responsible for the whole activity of RC42 to the governing bodies of ISA.





Organizes the holding of the General meetings of the Board of RC42.
Represents RC42 as an alternate on the Research Council of ISA.
Assists the President in organizing the work of the Board and the fulfillment of its decisions.
Collects the membership fees and other incoming fees.
Keeps financial reports and documentation.
Prepares the financial report of the Board to be accepted by the General Meeting;
Realizes the financial relations between RC42 and the ISA.





Gathers information for the development of social psychology (scientific activities, research projects, new publications, etc.).
Organizes the publications of the RC42 Newsletter and its distribution.
Ensures that at least two issues of the Newsletter appear per year.




Will take an active part within their region to:

bring new members to RC42
disseminate information on RC42 and its activities
locate researchers who could organize sessions and/or otherwise participate in the ISA World Congress and other ISA related conferences
identify new issues and creative lines of inquiry for further research on social psychology





These revised Statutes were proposed for review and acceptance at the Business Meeting of RC42 held in July 2006, at the General Meeting of RC42, ISA Congress in South Africa.
Amendments to statutes must be proposed to the Chair in writing at least 60 days prior to scheduled RC42 business meetings (every four years at World Congresses). Amendments are discussed at the business meetings and voted by simple majority of members present or voting in case of mail ballot.

title: Activities
text:
RC42 Activities
      
    

    
      
        
      

      
        July 4–10, 2027 • Gwangju, South Korea
        
        
        The XXI ISA World Congress of Sociology will take place in Gwangju, South Korea, from July 4 to 10, 2027, under the theme “Global Sociology in Turbulent Times.” The congress visual identity draws on Hangul and the cultural heritage of Gwangju, emphasizing inclusivity, connection, dialogue, diversity, and the coming together of global sociological voices.
        Read more →
      
    

    

      
        
          
        
        
          News
          
          Board updates, community news, and recent developments from RC42 Social Psychology.

title: Contributing to the RC42 Social Psychology Portal
text:
Thank you for your interest in contributing to the RC42 Social Psychology digital portal. This document explains how to propose changes, submit content, and participate in the editorial process.





The easiest way to contribute is through the forms on the portal:

Create a member profile — Add your profile to the members directory.
Submit a contribution — Share news, events, opportunities, resources, or blog posts.
Request a profile update — Update an existing profile.




You can also send contributions by email to isa-rc42@isa-sociology.org.



If you are familiar with GitHub:

Fork this repository.
Create a branch for your changes.
Make your edits (new .qmd files, data updates, etc.).
Submit a Pull Request with a clear description of your changes.
The RC42 board will review and merge approved changes.




Use the provided issue templates to:

Request a new member profile
Update an existing profile
Submit a news item
Submit an event
Submit an opportunity
Submit a resource













Type
Description




Member profile
Your academic profile for the members directory


News
Announcements relevant to the RC42 community


Event
Conferences, workshops, sessions related to sociological social psychology


Opportunity
Calls for papers, positions, fellowships, grants, awards


Resource
Publications, teaching materials, datasets, reading lists


Blog post
Essays or reflections on topics in sociological social psychology


Newsletter item
Content for the RC42 newsletter










Language: English.
Tone: Academic, professional, respectful.
Accuracy: Only submit factual, verifiable information. Do not fabricate or exaggerate.
Relevance: Contributions should be relevant to sociological social psychology and the RC42 community.
Brevity: Keep submissions concise. News items should be 100–300 words. Blog posts may be longer (500–2000 words).





Use clear, accessible language.
Provide links to original sources when applicable.
For publications, use a standard citation format (APA preferred).
Include dates in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) when possible.









You must provide explicit consent to have your profile published.
You choose what information to share publicly.
Your verification email is never published — it is used only for editorial contact.
You may request removal of your profile at any time by contacting the board.





By submitting content, you grant RC42 permission to publish it on this portal.
You retain authorship and may request removal at any time.
The RC42 board may edit contributions for clarity, length, or formatting.





Submitted data is stored only for the purpose of operating this portal.
No data is shared with third parties beyond what is published on the portal.
Form submissions via Netlify are stored according to Netlify’s privacy policy.







All submissions are reviewed by the RC42 board or a designated editor.
Nothing is published automatically — human review is required for every piece of content.
You will be contacted if clarification or revisions are needed.
The board reserves the right to decline submissions that do not meet editorial standards or are not relevant to the RC42 community.





Contributors are expected to:

Be respectful and professional in all communications.
Provide accurate and honest information.
Respect the privacy and intellectual property of others.
Follow the editorial guidelines described above.





Contact the RC42 board at isa-rc42@isa-sociology.org.

title: Contributing to the RC42 Social Psychology Portal
text:
Language: English.
Tone: Academic, professional, respectful.
Accuracy: Only submit factual, verifiable information. Do not fabricate or exaggerate.
Relevance: Contributions should be relevant to sociological social psychology and the RC42 community.
Brevity: Keep submissions concise. News items should be 100–300 words. Blog posts may be longer (500–2000 words).





Use clear, accessible language.
Provide links to original sources when applicable.
For publications, use a standard citation format (APA preferred).
Include dates in ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD) when possible.

title: Contributing to the RC42 Social Psychology Portal
text:
You must provide explicit consent to have your profile published.
You choose what information to share publicly.
Your verification email is never published — it is used only for editorial contact.
You may request removal of your profile at any time by contacting the board.





By submitting content, you grant RC42 permission to publish it on this portal.
You retain authorship and may request removal at any time.
The RC42 board may edit contributions for clarity, length, or formatting.





Submitted data is stored only for the purpose of operating this portal.
No data is shared with third parties beyond what is published on the portal.
Form submissions via Netlify are stored according to Netlify’s privacy policy.

title: Contributing to the RC42 Social Psychology Portal
text:
All submissions are reviewed by the RC42 board or a designated editor.
Nothing is published automatically — human review is required for every piece of content.
You will be contacted if clarification or revisions are needed.
The board reserves the right to decline submissions that do not meet editorial standards or are not relevant to the RC42 community.

title: Contributing to the RC42 Social Psychology Portal
text:
Contributors are expected to:

Be respectful and professional in all communications.
Provide accurate and honest information.
Respect the privacy and intellectual property of others.
Follow the editorial guidelines described above.

title: Events
text:
Conferences, workshops, panels, and sessions organized by or relevant to RC42 Social Psychology.




Congress


XXI ISA World Congress of Sociology 2027


The XXI ISA World Congress of Sociology is a major upcoming event. RC42 Social Psychology will participate with sessions organized by the Program Coordinator, Juan Carlos Castillo. Detailed session information will be published as it becomes available.


📅 Dates to be confirmed · 📍 Location to be confirmed



Additional events will be listed as they are submitted and approved. To submit an event, use the contribution form.





A listing of past RC42 events (conference sessions, workshops, etc.) will be added as information becomes available. If you have records of past RC42 events, please contact the board.





If you know of an event relevant to the RC42 community, please submit it through the contribution form.

title: Membership
text:
RC42 Social Psychology welcomes scholars and researchers from around the world who are interested in sociological social psychology.


Membership in RC42 is managed through the International Sociological Association (ISA). To become a member of RC42, you need to:

Be an ISA member — Join the International Sociological Association through the ISA website.
Select RC42 — When registering or renewing your ISA membership, select RC42 Social Psychology as one of your Research Committees.


ISA membership includes the option to join up to five Research Committees at no additional cost. Visit the ISA membership page for current fees and membership categories.




RC42 membership is open to:

Faculty members and researchers in sociology and related disciplines
Graduate students and early-career researchers
Independent scholars and practitioners with an interest in sociological social psychology
Researchers from any country or region





Participate in RC42 sessions at ISA World Congresses
Connect with an international network of scholars in sociological social psychology
Receive the RC42 newsletter
Be listed in the RC42 members directory (optional)
Submit and vote on proposals for RC42 activities
Stand for election to the RC42 board




In addition to your ISA membership, you can create a member profile on this portal to increase your visibility within the RC42 community.
Create a member profile →

Creating a profile on this portal is separate from ISA membership registration. This portal is a complementary resource and does not replace the ISA membership system.




For questions about RC42 membership, contact: isa-rc42@isa-sociology.org

title: Welcome to the RC42 Digital Portal
text:
RC42 Social Psychology is pleased to announce the launch of this complementary digital portal, designed to enhance communication, visibility, and collaboration among members worldwide.


This website serves as an extension of the official ISA RC42 page. It provides:

Members directory: A voluntary listing of RC42 members with their research profiles.
News and events: Announcements and upcoming activities relevant to the RC42 community.
Opportunities: Calls for papers, positions, fellowships, and other professional opportunities.
Newsletter archive: Past editions of the RC42 newsletter.
Resources: Publications, teaching materials, and research tools shared by members.
Participation pathways: Easy ways to contribute content and engage with the community.




We invite all RC42 members to:

Create a member profile — Share your research interests and connect with colleagues. Create profile →
Submit contributions — Share news, events, opportunities, or resources. Submit →
Provide feedback — Help us improve this portal. Contact us →




This portal will grow with contributions from the RC42 community. Upcoming developments may include:

Full member profiles for all board members
Event listings for the XXI ISA World Congress of Sociology 2027
An expanded newsletter archive
Shared resources and reading lists


All content on this portal is reviewed and approved by the RC42 board before publication. Nothing is published automatically without human review.

We look forward to building this resource together with the RC42 community.
— The RC42 Board 2023–2027

title: Opportunities
text:
This page lists calls for papers, job openings, fellowships, grants, awards, and other professional opportunities relevant to the RC42 Social Psychology community.

No opportunities are currently listed. Opportunities will be published here as they are submitted and approved by the RC42 board.




Calls for papers — Conference sessions, special issues, edited volumes
Positions — Faculty positions, postdoctoral fellowships, research positions
Fellowships & grants — Research funding, visiting fellowships, travel grants
Awards — Best paper awards, dissertation awards, career awards
Other — Workshops, summer schools, collaborative projects




If you know of an opportunity relevant to the RC42 community, please submit it through the contribution form.

All submissions are reviewed by the RC42 board before publication. Opportunities are listed as a service to the community and do not imply endorsement by RC42 or ISA.

title: Update Profile
text:
Profile updates are handled manually in this initial version of the portal. A self-service update form will be available in a future phase.



To update your existing member profile on the RC42 portal, please:

Send an email to isa-rc42@isa-sociology.org with:

Your full name (as it appears on your profile)
The specific information you want to update
The new content to replace the existing information

Or open a GitHub Issue (if you have a GitHub account):

Visit the RC42 repository (link to be updated)
Open a new issue using the “Update Profile” template
Fill in the requested information





You can request updates to any of the following:

Affiliation and position
Biography
Research interests
Selected publications
Academic links (ORCID, Google Scholar, LinkedIn, website)
Photo
Contact information
Country/city




Profile updates are processed by the RC42 board or designated editor. Please allow a few days for the update to be reflected on the portal.

In a future version of this portal, a self-service profile update form will be available, allowing members to submit updates directly through the website.

title: Board 2023–2027
text:
Leadership
        
        The current executive board coordinates RC42 initiatives, membership communication, and our active participation in International Sociological Association activities.

        
          Browse members directory
          View past boards since 1988 →
        
      

      
        
          Current Board
          
        

        
          
          
            
            
              
                
                  
                
                
                  President
                  
                  Universidad de Chile, Chile
                
              
              
              
                Professor at the Department of Sociology, Universidad de Chile, and Director of Research and Publications at the Faculty of Social Sciences. His work focuses on distributive justice, citizenship education, social cohesion, quantitative methods, structural equation modeling, multilevel analysis, and open science.

                  Citizenship education
                  Social cohesion
                  Open science
                  Quantitative methods

                  Secretary / Treasurer
                  
                  University of North Carolina at Charlotte, USA
                
              
              
              
                Professor of Sociology at UNC Charlotte. Her research focuses on small group interaction, nonverbal behaviors, identity, emotions, gender, expectations, and status processes.

                  University of Pretoria, South Africa
                
              
              
              
                Senior Lecturer in the Department of Sociology at the University of Pretoria. His research interests include race, identity, methodology, urban sociology, demography, and research methods.

                  Université catholique de Louvain, Belgium
                
              
              
              
                Researcher with a background in sociological social psychology and American studies. Her work focuses on activism, social movements, youth, subjectivation, and emotions in Latin American contexts.
